message:
refactor(core): Align types and config with centralized schema patterns

Synchronizes types.ts and config.ts with the evolving schema design:

## Types (src/types.ts)

- Extended ConfigSchema with additional optional fields for flexibility
- Refined type exports to support both runtime validation and TypeScript
- Added fallback defaults for scoring section fields
- Improved schema composition for nested configuration objects

## Config (src/config.ts)

Major simplification removing ~200 lines of duplicate code:

- REMOVED: Local interface definitions now imported from types.ts
- SIMPLIFIED: loadConfig() logic with cleaner cascade merge
- ADDED: getDefaultConfig() returning type-safe defaults
- ADDED: getUserConfigPath() for consistent path resolution
- ADDED: ensureConfigDirs() for atomic directory creation
- FIXED: Deep merge properly handles nested sanitization and scoring

### Config Loading Priority

1. Built-in defaults (from types.ts schema defaults)
2. Global user config (~/.cass-memory/config.json)
3. Repository config (.cass/config.yaml if present)
4. CLI overrides (passed programmatically)

Each layer only overrides explicitly set values, preserving defaults
for unspecified fields.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-0z1y","title":"[CLI:stats] renderStatsUI - Pretty terminal output","description":"Implements the renderStatsUI function that creates beautiful terminal output for playbook statistics.

FUNCTION SIGNATURE:
```typescript
function renderStatsUI(stats: PlaybookStats): string
```

OUTPUT FORMAT:
```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│              CASS-MEMORY PLAYBOOK HEALTH            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Total Bullets: 156 (Global: 89, Workspace: 67)      │
│                                                     │
│ BY MATURITY:                                        │
│   ● Candidate:   23 (14.7%)  ░░░░░                  │
│   ● Established: 87 (55.8%)  ████████████████       │
│   ● Proven:      41 (26.3%)  ████████               │
│   ● Deprecated:   5 ( 3.2%)  █                      │
│                                                     │
│ BY TYPE:                                            │
│   ● Rules:         142 (91.0%)                      │
│   ● Anti-Patterns:  14 ( 9.0%)                      │
│                                                     │
│ SCORE DISTRIBUTION:                                 │
│   🌟 Excellent (8+):  28 (17.9%)                    │
│   ✓  Good (4-7):      94 (60.3%)                    │
│   –  Neutral (0-3):   29 (18.6%)                    │
│   ⚠  At Risk (<0):     5 ( 3.2%)                    │
│                                                     │
│ TOP PERFORMERS:                                     │
│   1. [12.4] Use React.memo for expensive renders   │
│   2. [11.8] Run migrations in transaction          │
│   3. [10.2] Validate input at API boundary         │
│                                                     │
│ ⚠  AT RISK (5):                                     │
│   • [-2.1] abc-123: Cache without expiry           │
│   • [-1.5] def-456: Skip error handling            │
│                                                     │
│ 📊 STALE BULLETS (12): No feedback in 90+ days     │
│ 🔗 MERGE CANDIDATES (3): High similarity detected  │
│                                                     │
│ 💡 RECOMMENDATIONS:                                 │
│   • Review at-risk bullets: cass-memory show <id>  │
│   • Consider merging similar rules                 │
│   • Clean up stale bullets                         │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS:

1. BOX DRAWING:
   - Use Unicode box characters (╭╮╰╯├┤│─)
   - Dynamic width based on terminal size
   - Proper padding and alignment

2. COLOR CODING (using chalk):
   - Green: Good metrics, top performers
   - Yellow: Neutral, warnings
   - Red: At risk, negative scores
   - Blue: Headers, section titles
   - Gray: Secondary information

3. VISUAL ELEMENTS:
   - Bar charts using █░ characters
   - Percentage display with proper formatting
   - Icons/emoji for quick scanning
   - Proper number formatting (1,234 vs 1234)

4. RESPONSIVE LAYOUT:
   - Detect terminal width
   - Truncate long bullet previews with ellipsis
   - Adjust bar chart length to fit
   - Collapse sections if terminal too narrow

5. INFORMATION HIERARCHY:
   - Most important metrics at top
   - Actionable items highlighted
   - Supporting details below
   - Recommendations at bottom

CONTENT PRIORITIZATION:
- Show top 3 performers (not all 10)
- Show all at-risk bullets (critical)
- Summarize stale/merge counts (details via other commands)
- Include next-action recommendations

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Works in non-color terminals (fallback to ASCII)
- Readable without emoji support
- Screen reader friendly (avoid pure visual elements)

DEPENDENCIES:
- chalk (colors)
- terminal-size (width detection)
- String utilities (truncate, pad, align)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Output fits in standard 80-column terminal
- Colors enhance readability without being essential
- All key metrics visible at a glance
- Actionable insights clearly highlighted
- Professional appearance suitable for demos","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:54:03.844591-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:54:03.844591-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-14xw","title":"[Docs] CONTRIBUTING.md - Contribution guidelines","description":"Create CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution guidelines for developers.

Sections:
1. Development Setup:
   - Prerequisites: Node.js 18+, Bun, Git
   - Clone repository
   - Install dependencies: bun install
   - Run in development: bun run dev
   
2. Running Tests:
   - Unit tests: bun test
   - Integration tests: bun test:integration
   - Coverage: bun test --coverage
   
3. Code Style:
   - TypeScript strict mode enabled
   - Prettier for formatting
   - ESLint for linting
   - Run: bun run lint && bun run format
   
4. PR Process:
   - Fork repository
   - Create feature branch
   - Write tests for new features
   - Ensure all tests pass
   - Update documentation
   - Submit PR with clear description
   
5. Issue Templates:
   - Bug reports
   - Feature requests
   - Documentation improvements
   
6. Architecture Overview:
   - Brief explanation for contributors
   - Link to detailed architecture docs

Code of Conduct: Be respectful and constructive","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:46.118799-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:52:03.623278-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-1ce","title":"[Playbook] getBulletsByCategory - Filter bullets by category","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function getBulletsByCategory(
  playbook: Playbook,
  category: string,
  includeDeprecated?: boolean
): PlaybookBullet[]
```

## Purpose
Filter bullets by category with optional inclusion of deprecated bullets. Returns sorted array by effective score.

## Algorithm
1. Filter playbook.bullets by category match
2. If includeDeprecated is false (default):
   - Filter out bullets with state === "retired"
   - Filter out bullets with maturity === "deprecated"
3. Calculate effective score for each bullet
4. Sort by effective score (descending)
5. Return sorted array

## Category Matching
- **Exact match**: category === bullet.category
- **Case-insensitive**: Normalize to lowercase
- **Common categories**: "testing", "security", "performance", "code-quality", "documentation"

## Effective Score Sorting
Priority order:
1. Highest score first (most helpful)
2. Recent validation (newer lastValidatedAt)
3. Maturity level (proven > established > candidate)

## includeDeprecated Parameter
```typescript
// Active bullets only (default)
const testing = getBulletsByCategory(playbook, "testing");

// Include deprecated for historical analysis
const allTesting = getBulletsByCategory(playbook, "testing", true);
```

## Usage Scenarios
1. **Context generation**: Get top N rules for category
2. **Category stats**: Count bullets per category
3. **Markdown export**: Group bullets by category
4. **Search**: Find rules in specific domain

## Implementation Notes
- Case-insensitive category matching
- Sort by effective score for relevance
- Default excludes deprecated (most common use)
- Return empty array if no matches

## Return Value
```typescript
PlaybookBullet[]  // Sorted by effective score DESC
```

## Dependencies
- calculateEffectiveScore() - for sorting
- getActiveBullets() - similar filtering

## Related Functions
- getActiveBullets() - all active bullets
- exportToMarkdown() - uses this for grouping
- findMergeCandidates() - groups by category first","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:10.558681-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:24.45406-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-1f7","title":"[Playbook] savePlaybook - Write playbook to YAML","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
async function savePlaybook(playbook: Playbook, config: Config): Promise<void>
```

## Purpose
Write playbook to YAML file atomically. Updates metadata and prevents corruption through atomic write strategy.

## Algorithm
1. Update metadata.lastReflection to now()
2. Ensure parent directory exists (mkdir -p)
3. Stringify with yaml.stringify()
4. Write atomically:
   - Write to `{playbookPath}.tmp`
   - Rename to `{playbookPath}` (atomic operation)
5. Update metadata.totalReflections if changed

## Atomic Write Strategy
**Critical**: Never write directly to playbook.yaml
- Write to temporary file first
- Rename is atomic on POSIX systems
- Prevents corruption if process crashes mid-write
- Ensures playbook is always in valid state

## Metadata Updates
- `lastReflection`: Always updated to current timestamp
- `totalReflections`: Incremented if bullets changed
- `totalSessionsProcessed`: Updated by curator

## Implementation Notes
- Use fs.promises.rename() for atomic rename
- Use yaml.stringify() with proper formatting
- Ensure directory exists before writing
- Handle EACCES permission errors

## Error Handling
- **EACCES**: Permission denied on directory or file
- **ENOSPC**: No space left on device
- **EIO**: I/O errors during write

## Dependencies
- fs/promises
- yaml library
- path module for directory handling

## Related Functions
- loadPlaybook() - reads what this writes
- All mutation functions - call this to persist changes","notes":"Existing playbook helper implementations already present; no code changes made. Released reservation; feel free to take over.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:46.901077-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:56:58.848398-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-1gub","title":"[Docs] Error messages and troubleshooting guide","description":"Document common error messages and troubleshooting solutions.

Common errors:
- 'cass not found' → Installation instructions for cass CLI prerequisite
- 'API key missing' → How to set OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variables
- 'Playbook corrupt' → Recovery instructions using validate and backup restoration
- 'Rate limited' → Explanation of exponential backoff and retry logic
- 'Permission denied' → chmod +x instructions for binary
- 'Config file invalid' → JSON/YAML validation and schema errors
- 'SQLite locked' → Concurrent access issues and solutions
- 'Session file not found' → Path configuration and session discovery

For each error:
- Error message text
- Root cause explanation
- Step-by-step solution
- Prevention tips

Include diagnostic commands like 'doctor' for automatic troubleshooting","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:40.351434-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:57.214644-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-1h9","title":"[Diary] extractDiarySafe - Extraction with fallback","description":"Safe wrapper around diary extraction with graceful fallback on failure.

Function Signature:
async function extractDiarySafe(
  sessionContent: string,
  metadata: SessionMetadata,
  config: Config
): Promise<DiaryEntry>

Process:
1. Try calling extractDiaryFields(sessionContent, metadata, config)
2. On success: Return full DiaryEntry with all extracted fields
3. On failure: Return minimal fallback DiaryEntry

Fallback DiaryEntry Structure:
- status: 'mixed'
- accomplishments: ['[Extraction failed - see raw session]']
- challenges: ['Diary extraction error: {error.message}']
- decisions: []
- preferences: []
- keyLearnings: []
- suggestedTags: ['extraction-failure']
- sessionPath: metadata.sessionPath
- agent: metadata.agent
- workspace: metadata.workspace
- timestamp: current time

Key Features:
- Never crashes on LLM failure
- Preserves session reference even on failure
- Error message stored as challenge
- Enables post-mortem debugging
- Maintains diary continuity

Error Scenarios Handled:
- LLM API timeout or rate limit
- Invalid JSON response from LLM
- Schema validation failure
- Network errors
- Prompt token limit exceeded

Philosophy:
A minimal diary is better than no diary. Failed extractions are marked clearly but don't break the pipeline.

Output:
Always returns valid DiaryEntry, either full extraction or minimal fallback.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:25.16681-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:35.706793-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-1jdn","title":"[Foundation] Define ~/.cass-memory/ directory structure","description":"Define and implement the ~/.cass-memory/ global directory structure for user-level CASS Memory System data.

## Context
The ~/.cass-memory/ directory is the user's global memory store, containing personal configuration, playbooks, diary entries, and usage tracking. This directory persists across all projects and repositories the user works on, serving as their personal agent memory layer.

## Technical Specification

### Directory Structure:
~/.cass-memory/
├── config.json              # User configuration (API keys, default model, etc.)
├── playbook.yaml            # Personal global playbook (rules across all projects)
├── toxic_bullets.log        # Permanently blocked patterns (user-level)
├── diary/                   # Diary entries organized by date
│   ├── 2025-12-07.md
│   ├── 2025-12-06.md
│   └── ...
├── reflections/             # Processed session tracking
│   ├── session-abc123.json
│   ├── session-def456.json
│   └── ...
├── usage.jsonl              # Bullet usage log with timestamps
├── embeddings/              # Local vector store for semantic search
│   ├── index.bin
│   └── metadata.json
└── cost/                    # LLM cost tracking
    ├── monthly-2025-12.jsonl
    └── total.json

### File Specifications:

#### config.json:
- llm_provider: 'openai' | 'anthropic' | 'google'
- model: string (e.g., 'gpt-4', 'claude-opus-4-5', 'gemini-pro')
- api_key: string (primary API key)
- api_keys: { openai?: string, anthropic?: string, google?: string }
- max_tokens: number (default response length)
- temperature: number (0-1, creativity setting)
- enable_curation: boolean (auto-curate bullets)
- evidence_threshold: number (usage count before LLM curation)
- enable_sanitization: boolean (auto-detect secrets)

#### playbook.yaml:
- bullets: Array of global rules (applies to ALL projects)
- categories: SETUP, BUILD, TEST, DEBUG, DEPLOY, etc.
- each bullet has: content, context, priority, state, usage_count

#### toxic_bullets.log:
- Newline-delimited list of blocked bullet content
- Case-insensitive matching
- Applied globally to prevent toxic rules

#### diary/ (daily markdown files):
- One file per day: YYYY-MM-DD.md
- Timestamped entries
- Free-form text + metadata frontmatter
- Used for reflection and pattern extraction

#### reflections/ (session metadata):
- One JSON file per session
- session_id, timestamp, repo_path, command, duration
- extracted_patterns: Array of learned behaviors
- applied_bullets: Array of bullets used in session

#### usage.jsonl (bullet tracking):
- One line per bullet usage
- timestamp, bullet_hash, repo_path, session_id, outcome
- Used for evidence-based curation
- Queryable for historical analysis

#### embeddings/ (vector store):
- Binary index for fast semantic search
- Metadata mapping IDs to bullet content
- Future: enable natural language playbook queries

#### cost/ (LLM usage tracking):
- Monthly JSONL files: timestamp, provider, model, tokens, cost
- total.json: cumulative spend across all providers
- Helps users budget LLM usage

## Reasoning

### Why Global Directory:
1. Cross-Project Memory: Rules learned in one project apply to others
2. User Ownership: User controls their personal memory store
3. Privacy: Personal diary and usage data stays local
4. Portability: Backup and restore entire memory with one directory

### Why config.json:
1. Single Source of Truth: All LLM configuration in one place
2. API Key Security: Stored locally, never committed to git
3. User Preferences: Default model and behavior settings
4. Easy Onboarding: cass-memory init walks through setup

### Why playbook.yaml:
1. Human-Readable: YAML is easy to edit manually
2. Version-Controllable: Can commit to dotfiles repo
3. Mergeable: Combines with repo-level playbooks
4. Hierarchical: Categories organize rules logically

### Why toxic_bullets.log:
1. Safety Net: Prevents re-learning bad patterns
2. Permanent: Once blocked, stays blocked
3. User Control: Users curate their own blocklist
4. Simple Format: Plain text, easy to inspect/edit

### Why diary/:
1. Human-Readable: Markdown for manual review
2. Daily Organization: Natural time-based structure
3. Reflection Source: LLM mines diary for patterns
4. Debugging: Understand what agent was thinking

### Why reflections/:
1. Session Tracking: Know what agent learned when
2. Reproducibility: Trace which bullets were applied
3. Analytics: Measure bullet effectiveness
4. Audit Trail: Compliance and debugging

### Why usage.jsonl:
1. Evidence-Based: Track which rules are actually useful
2. Queryable: Filter by repo, time range, outcome
3. Curation Input: LLM uses usage data for decisions
4. Append-Only: Fast writes, no corruption risk

### Why embeddings/:
1. Semantic Search: Find similar rules without exact matches
2. Local-First: No cloud dependency for search
3. Future-Proof: Enables natural language queries
4. Privacy: Embeddings stay on user's machine

### Why cost/:
1. Budget Tracking: Know how much LLM usage costs
2. Provider Comparison: See which provider is most cost-effective
3. Monthly Reports: Track spending trends
4. Transparency: Users see exactly what they're paying

## Acceptance Criteria

- ~/.cass-memory/ directory is created on cass-memory init
- All subdirectories created (diary, reflections, embeddings, cost)
- config.json has sensible defaults if not present
- playbook.yaml created with empty bullets array
- toxic_bullets.log created (empty file)
- usage.jsonl created (empty file)
- Permissions set correctly (user read/write only for config.json)
- Directory creation is idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
- Helper functions in utils.ts: resolveGlobalDir(), ensureGlobalStructure()
- cass-memory doctor verifies directory structure

## How This Serves the Universal Agent Memory System

The ~/.cass-memory/ directory is the user's personal memory layer:

1. Persistence: Memory survives across projects, jobs, and machines
2. Privacy: Personal diary and usage data never leaves user's machine
3. Portability: Backup entire memory with one directory
4. Cross-Project Learning: Rules learned in project A apply to project B
5. User Control: Users own and curate their memory
6. Multi-Agent Support: Future agents can read from same memory store

This global directory is the foundation for the universal agent memory vision. Without it, agents would be memoryless, relearning the same patterns in every project.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:22.033613-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:22.033613-05:00"}
@@ -157,7 +157,7 @@
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-dwew","title":"[Config] loadMergedPlaybook - Combine global + repo","description":"Function signature:
```typescript
async function loadMergedPlaybook(config: Config): Promise<Playbook>
```

Steps:
1. Load global playbook (~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml)
2. Load repo playbook (./.cass/playbook.yaml) - may not exist
3. Merge bullets:
   - Same ID → repo wins
   - Different IDs → keep both (dedupe by content)
4. Load toxic logs from both levels
5. Filter bullets against combined toxic log
6. Return merged playbook

Edge cases:
- Repo playbook may not exist (use global only)
- Both playbooks may have same bullet ID (repo wins)
- Content-based deduplication to avoid semantic duplicates
- Toxic log filtering happens AFTER merge

Returns: Combined playbook with repo-level overrides applied","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:38.937664-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:38.937664-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-e21","title":"[Security] Sanitization application points documentation","description":"Document WHERE to apply the sanitize() function throughout the codebase.

Application points (in order of data flow):
1. After cassExport, BEFORE passing to LLM (in diary generation)
   - Raw session content from CASS is untrusted
   - Must sanitize before LLM sees it

2. Before writing DiaryEntry to disk
   - Prevent secrets from persisting in diary files
   - Apply sanitize() to entry.content

3. Before storing content in PlaybookBullet
   - Sanitize before creating bullet objects
   - Apply to bullet.content and bullet.evidence

4. In context output (should already be sanitized if stored sanitized)
   - Defense in depth: verify sanitization at output time
   - Shouldn't find anything if earlier stages worked

GOLDEN RULE: NEVER pass unsanitized session content to LLM or disk

Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Sanitize in diary generation flow
- [ ] Sanitize before DiaryEntry persistence
- [ ] Sanitize before PlaybookBullet creation
- [ ] Add verification at context output

Priority: 0 (Security Critical - P12)","notes":"Added sanitization application points under README Security/Secret Sanitization; communicated to agents. No sanitize.ts changes to avoid conflict with WhiteCat reservation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:49.167852-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.200679-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:45:48.046117-05:00"}

-{"id":"cass_memory_system-e8x","title":"[Playbook] loadPlaybook - Load and validate playbook from YAML","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
async function loadPlaybook(config: Config): Promise<Playbook>
```

## Purpose
Load and validate playbook from YAML file. Handles missing files, corruption, and schema validation with automatic recovery.

## Algorithm
1. Expand playbookPath from config
2. Check if file exists
3. If not exists: create empty playbook and return
4. Read file content
5. If empty: create new playbook and return
6. Parse YAML content
7. Normalize keys (snake_case → camelCase for compatibility)
8. Validate with PlaybookSchema.safeParse()
9. If invalid: backup to .backup.{timestamp} and create new
10. Return validated playbook

## Error Handling
- **ENOENT**: File doesn't exist → create empty
- **EACCES**: Permission denied → throw error
- **Parse errors**: YAML syntax errors → backup and recreate
- **Validation errors**: Schema mismatch → backup and recreate

## Backup Strategy
When corruption detected:
- Backup to: `{playbookPath}.backup.{timestamp}`
- Log warning with backup location
- Create fresh empty playbook
- Never lose user data

## Implementation Notes
- Use fs.promises for async file operations
- Use yaml.parse() for YAML parsing
- Normalize keys for backward compatibility
- Always validate against current schema version

## Dependencies
- fs/promises
- yaml library
- PlaybookSchema (Zod schema)
- createEmptyPlaybook()

## Related Functions
- createEmptyPlaybook() - creates new structure
- savePlaybook() - writes loaded playbook back","notes":"Existing playbook helper implementations already present; no code changes made. Released reservation; feel free to take over.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:45.760512-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:56:58.85679-05:00"}
+{"id":"cass_memory_system-e8x","title":"[Playbook] loadPlaybook - Load and validate playbook from YAML","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
async function loadPlaybook(config: Config): Promise<Playbook>
```

## Purpose
Load and validate playbook from YAML file. Handles missing files, corruption, and schema validation with automatic recovery.

## Algorithm
1. Expand playbookPath from config
2. Check if file exists
3. If not exists: create empty playbook and return
4. Read file content
5. If empty: create new playbook and return
6. Parse YAML content
7. Normalize keys (snake_case → camelCase for compatibility)
8. Validate with PlaybookSchema.safeParse()
9. If invalid: backup to .backup.{timestamp} and create new
10. Return validated playbook

## Error Handling
- **ENOENT**: File doesn't exist → create empty
- **EACCES**: Permission denied → throw error
- **Parse errors**: YAML syntax errors → backup and recreate
- **Validation errors**: Schema mismatch → backup and recreate

## Backup Strategy
When corruption detected:
- Backup to: `{playbookPath}.backup.{timestamp}`
- Log warning with backup location
- Create fresh empty playbook
- Never lose user data

## Implementation Notes
- Use fs.promises for async file operations
- Use yaml.parse() for YAML parsing
- Normalize keys for backward compatibility
- Always validate against current schema version

## Dependencies
- fs/promises
- yaml library
- PlaybookSchema (Zod schema)
- createEmptyPlaybook()

## Related Functions
- createEmptyPlaybook() - creates new structure
- savePlaybook() - writes loaded playbook back","notes":"Existing playbook helper implementations already present; no code changes made.  {"id":"cass_memory_system-efd","title":"[Types] PlaybookBullet Schema Part 1 - Identity and Scope","description":"## Overview
Define identity and scope fields for PlaybookBullet schema. These fields establish bullet uniqueness and applicability hierarchy.

## Identity Field

### id: string
- Format: 'b-{timestamp36}-{random}'
- Example: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8'
- Generation: timestamp in base36 + 4-char random suffix
- Purpose: Globally unique, sortable identifier
- Immutable: Never changes after creation

## Scope Hierarchy (5 Levels)

### scope: enum
Type: 'global' | 'workspace' | 'language' | 'framework' | 'task'

Hierarchy (broadest to narrowest):

1. global - Universal rules (e.g., 'Write clear commit messages')
   - Applies: Always
   - scopeKey: Not used
   - workspace: Not used

2. workspace - Repository-specific rules
   - Applies: When in matching workspace directory
   - scopeKey: Not used (workspace field holds path)
   - workspace: '/path/to/project'
   - Example: 'Use AuthService from @/lib/auth in this project'

3. language - Language-specific rules
   - Applies: When working in files of that language
   - scopeKey: Language identifier (e.g., 'typescript', 'python', 'rust')
   - workspace: Optional (can be workspace + language)
   - Example: 'For TypeScript, prefer interfaces over types for objects'

4. framework - Framework/library-specific rules
   - Applies: When project uses that framework
   - scopeKey: Framework identifier (e.g., 'nextjs', 'react', 'django')
   - workspace: Optional
   - Example: 'For Next.js, use app router not pages router'

5. task - Task-type specific rules
   - Applies: When performing that category of work
   - scopeKey: Task category (e.g., 'authentication', 'testing', 'api-design')
   - workspace: Optional
   - Example: 'For authentication tasks, validate JWT expiration'

## Scope Filtering Logic
When retrieving bullets for context:
1. Include all 'global' scope bullets
2. If in workspace, include matching 'workspace' bullets
3. If file language detected, include matching 'language' bullets
4. If framework detected, include matching 'framework' bullets
5. If task category inferred, include matching 'task' bullets

Multiple scopes can apply simultaneously (e.g., workspace + language + task).

## Fields

### Required
- id: string - Unique identifier
- scope: 'global' | 'workspace' | 'language' | 'framework' | 'task'

### Optional
- scopeKey: string | undefined
  - Used for: language, framework, task scopes
  - Not used for: global, workspace scopes
  - Examples: 'typescript', 'react', 'testing'

- workspace: string | undefined
  - Used for: workspace scope (required)
  - Optional for: other scopes (adds workspace constraint)
  - Value: Absolute path to workspace root
  - Example: '/Users/dev/projects/my-app'

## Examples

Global Rule: { id: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8', scope: 'global', content: 'Write descriptive commit messages' }

Workspace Rule: { id: 'b-m8n3p5q7-b4g9', scope: 'workspace', workspace: '/Users/dev/projects/auth-service', content: 'Use TokenValidator from @/lib/auth for all token checks' }

Language Rule: { id: 'b-n9o4q6r8-c5h0', scope: 'language', scopeKey: 'typescript', content: 'Prefer const assertions over enums for string literals' }

Framework Rule: { id: 'b-o0p5r7s9-d6i1', scope: 'framework', scopeKey: 'nextjs', workspace: '/Users/dev/projects/web-app', content: 'Use server actions in app directory, not API routes' }

Task Rule: { id: 'b-p1q6s8t0-e7j2', scope: 'task', scopeKey: 'testing', content: 'Use vitest for unit tests, playwright for e2e' }

## Acceptance Criteria
- id field with format specification
- scope enum with 5 values
- scopeKey optional field
- workspace optional field
- ID generation utility function
- Scope filtering logic implemented
- Examples for all 5 scope types
- JSDoc explaining hierarchy","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:58.006278-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:45.47468-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ehaq","title":"[CLI:stats] computeFullStats - Calculate all metrics","description":"Implements the computeFullStats function that calculates comprehensive playbook metrics.

FUNCTION SIGNATURE:
```typescript
interface PlaybookStats {
  total: number;
  byScope: { global: number; workspace: number };
  byMaturity: { 
    candidate: number; 
    established: number; 
    proven: number; 
    deprecated: number 
  };
  byType: { rule: number; antiPattern: number };
  scoreDistribution: { 
    excellent: number;    // score >= 8
    good: number;         // score 4-7
    neutral: number;      // score 0-3
    atRisk: number;       // score < 0
  };
  topPerformers: { 
    id: string; 
    score: number; 
    preview: string 
  }[];
  atRisk: { 
    id: string; 
    score: number; 
    reason: string 
  }[];
  stale: { 
    id: string; 
    daysSinceLastFeedback: number 
  }[];
  mergeCandidates: { 
    ids: string[];        // Array of 2+ similar bullet IDs
    similarity: number;   // Semantic similarity score
  }[];
}

function computeFullStats(playbook: Playbook): PlaybookStats
```

COMPUTATION LOGIC:

1. BASIC COUNTS:
   - Iterate all bullets, count by scope/maturity/type
   - Skip deprecated unless explicitly included

2. SCORE DISTRIBUTION:
   - Calculate effective score (with decay) for each bullet
   - Bucket into excellent/good/neutral/atRisk
   - Use consistent thresholds across system

3. TOP PERFORMERS:
   - Sort by effective score descending
   - Take top 10
   - Generate preview (first 60 chars of content)

4. AT RISK BULLETS:
   - Filter bullets with score < 0
   - Include reason: "Negative feedback" or "Declining effectiveness"
   - Sort by score ascending (worst first)

5. STALE BULLETS:
   - Calculate days since last helpful/harmful feedback
   - Filter bullets with >90 days (configurable threshold)
   - Include only active bullets (not deprecated)

6. MERGE CANDIDATES:
   - Compute pairwise semantic similarity (if embeddings available)
   - Find pairs/groups with similarity > 0.90
   - Exclude already-merged or deprecated bullets
   - Limit to top 5 groups

PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATIONS:
- Cache embeddings for similarity computation
- Use single pass for multiple aggregations
- Lazy compute merge candidates (expensive)
- Parallel score calculations for large playbooks

EDGE CASES:
- Empty playbook → return zeros
- No embeddings → skip merge candidates
- All bullets deprecated → show warning
- No feedback data → mark all as stale

DEPENDENCIES:
- Playbook data structure
- Score calculation utilities
- Embedding similarity (optional)
- Date/time utilities for staleness

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- All metrics accurate and consistent
- Performance <500ms for 1000 bullets
- Handles edge cases gracefully
- Stats match reality when spot-checked","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:53:34.560049-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:53:34.560049-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qef","title":"[Decay] isStale - Detect rules without recent feedback","description":"Detect rules that haven't received any feedback (helpful or harmful) in a configurable period. Stale rules need review - they may be obsolete or just unused.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Check if bullet is stale (no recent feedback).
 * Stale rules may be obsolete, irrelevant, or just not being invoked.
 */
function isStale(bullet: PlaybookBullet, staleDays: number = 90): boolean {
  const allEvents = [...bullet.helpfulEvents, ...bullet.harmfulEvents];
  
  if (allEvents.length === 0) {
    // No feedback ever - check creation date
    return daysSince(bullet.createdAt) > staleDays;
  }

  const lastFeedback = Math.max(
    ...allEvents.map(e => new Date(e.timestamp).getTime())
  );
  
  return daysSince(lastFeedback) > staleDays;
}

function daysSince(timestamp: string | number): number {
  const time = typeof timestamp === 'string' 
    ? new Date(timestamp).getTime() 
    : timestamp;
  return (Date.now() - time) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);
}
```

STALENESS LOGIC:

Case 1: No feedback ever
- Check days since bullet.createdAt
- If > staleDays, consider stale
- May indicate rule never matches or is too narrow

Case 2: Has feedback history
- Find most recent event (helpful OR harmful)
- If last event > staleDays ago, consider stale
- May indicate codebase evolved past this rule

DEFAULT: 90 days
- Configurable per playbook or system-wide
- Shorter for fast-moving projects
- Longer for stable, mature codebases

WHY STALENESS MATTERS:

STALE \!= BAD:
- Stale rule might be perfect but just not encountered
- Could be edge case that rarely triggers
- Or could be obsolete cruft from old codebase

STALE RULES NEED REVIEW:
- Human review: Is this still relevant?
- If yes: Keep or pin
- If no: Deprecate or delete
- If unsure: Leave as candidate, watch for another 90 days

EXAMPLE SCENARIOS:

1. SECURITY RULE (stale but critical):
   - "Never commit AWS credentials"
   - Created 1 year ago
   - 0 feedback (good thing\!)
   - Stale = true but should be pinned
   - Keep forever even without feedback

2. OBSOLETE FRAMEWORK RULE:
   - "Use Angular 1.x controllers"
   - Last helpful 300 days ago
   - Project migrated to React
   - Stale = true, should deprecate

3. EDGE CASE RULE:
   - "Handle leap year in date calculations"
   - Created 200 days ago
   - 0 feedback (hasn't been encountered)
   - Stale = true but may still be valid
   - Review and decide

INTEGRATION:

Used by stats command:
```typescript
const staleCount = bullets.filter(b => isStale(b)).length;
console.log(`Stale rules (>90 days): ${staleCount}`);
```

Used by review command (future):
```typescript
const staleRules = bullets.filter(b => isStale(b) && \!b.pinned);
console.log('Rules needing review:', staleRules);
```

CONFIGURATION:
- Default staleDays: 90
- Override per-playbook in playbook.json
- Override per-invocation: isStale(bullet, 60)

DEPENDENCIES:
- daysSince() helper
- PlaybookBullet.createdAt field
- PlaybookBullet.helpfulEvents array
- PlaybookBullet.harmfulEvents array

RETURNS:
- true: No feedback in staleDays period
- false: Has recent feedback","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:10.631857-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:44.185002-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qm27","title":"[Decay] applyMaturityTransitions - Batch promotion/demotion","description":"Apply promotion/demotion rules across all bullets in a playbook. Called at the end of each curate cycle to automatically advance proven rules and demote toxic ones. Returns summary of all transitions.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Apply maturity transitions across entire playbook.
 * Checks each bullet for promotion/demotion and applies changes.
 * Called at end of curate cycle.
 */
function applyMaturityTransitions(playbook: Playbook): {
  promotions: { bulletId: string; from: string; to: string }[];
  demotions: { bulletId: string; from: string; to: string }[];
  autoDeprecated: { bulletId: string; score: number }[];
  unchanged: number;
} {
  const promotions: { bulletId: string; from: string; to: string }[] = [];
  const demotions: { bulletId: string; from: string; to: string }[] = [];
  const autoDeprecated: { bulletId: string; score: number }[] = [];
  let unchanged = 0;

  playbook.bullets.forEach(bullet => {
    if (bullet.maturity === 'deprecated') {
      unchanged++;
      return;  // Skip already deprecated
    }

    const currentMaturity = bullet.maturity;
    
    // Check for promotion first
    const promotionTarget = checkForPromotion(bullet);
    if (promotionTarget !== currentMaturity) {
      bullet.maturity = promotionTarget;
      promotions.push({
        bulletId: bullet.id,
        from: currentMaturity,
        to: promotionTarget
      });
      return;
    }

    // Check for demotion
    const demotionTarget = checkForDemotion(bullet);
    if (demotionTarget === 'auto-deprecate') {
      const score = getEffectiveScore(bullet);
      bullet.maturity = 'deprecated';
      bullet.deprecatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
      bullet.deprecationReason = `Auto-deprecated due to negative score (${score.toFixed(2)})`;
      autoDeprecated.push({ bulletId: bullet.id, score });
      return;
    }
    
    if (demotionTarget !== currentMaturity) {
      bullet.maturity = demotionTarget;
      demotions.push({
        bulletId: bullet.id,
        from: currentMaturity,
        to: demotionTarget
      });
      return;
    }

    unchanged++;
  });

  return {
    promotions,
    demotions,
    autoDeprecated,
    unchanged
  };
}
```

TRANSITION RULES:

Processing Order:
1. Skip deprecated bullets (already terminal state)
2. Check promotion first (reward positive signals)
3. Check demotion only if no promotion
4. Apply auto-deprecation if score < -3

Why Promotion First?
- Positive signals should override minor negatives
- Prevents flip-flopping between maturity levels
- Rewards consistent helpful marks

EXAMPLE SCENARIOS:

Scenario 1: Mass Promotion
```typescript
// After successful sprint with good playbook usage
const result = applyMaturityTransitions(playbook);
// Result:
// promotions: 5 (candidate → established)
// demotions: 0
// autoDeprecated: 0
// unchanged: 37
```

Scenario 2: Codebase Evolution
```typescript
// After major refactor, old rules become toxic
const result = applyMaturityTransitions(playbook);
// Result:
// promotions: 2 (new patterns emerging)
// demotions: 8 (old patterns harmful)
// autoDeprecated: 3 (severely toxic old rules)
// unchanged: 29
```

Scenario 3: Stable Playbook
```typescript
// Mature playbook with infrequent changes
const result = applyMaturityTransitions(playbook);
// Result:
// promotions: 0
// demotions: 0
// autoDeprecated: 0
// unchanged: 42
```

OUTPUT STRUCTURE:

Promotions:
```json
{
  "bulletId": "rule-123",
  "from": "candidate",
  "to": "established"
}
```

Demotions:
```json
{
  "bulletId": "rule-456",
  "from": "proven",
  "to": "established"
}
```

Auto-Deprecated:
```json
{
  "bulletId": "rule-789",
  "score": -5.25
}
```

INTEGRATION WITH CURATE CYCLE:
```typescript
// At end of curate command
async function finalizeCuration(playbook: Playbook) {
  // 1. Save all feedback events
  await savePlaybook(playbook);

  // 2. Apply maturity transitions
  const transitions = applyMaturityTransitions(playbook);

  // 3. Save updated maturities
  await savePlaybook(playbook);

  // 4. Report to user
  if (transitions.promotions.length > 0) {
    console.log(`✓ Promoted ${transitions.promotions.length} rules`);
    transitions.promotions.forEach(p => {
      console.log(`  - ${p.bulletId}: ${p.from} → ${p.to}`);
    });
  }

  if (transitions.demotions.length > 0) {
    console.log(`⚠ Demoted ${transitions.demotions.length} rules`);
    transitions.demotions.forEach(d => {
      console.log(`  - ${d.bulletId}: ${d.from} → ${d.to}`);
    });
  }

  if (transitions.autoDeprecated.length > 0) {
    console.log(`⛔ Auto-deprecated ${transitions.autoDeprecated.length} rules`);
    transitions.autoDeprecated.forEach(a => {
      console.log(`  - ${a.bulletId} (score: ${a.score.toFixed(2)})`);
    });
  }
}
```

SIDE EFFECTS:
- Modifies playbook.bullets[].maturity in place
- Sets deprecatedAt timestamp for auto-deprecated bullets
- Sets deprecationReason for auto-deprecated bullets
- Does NOT save to disk (caller's responsibility)

IDEMPOTENCY:
Safe to call multiple times - already-transitioned bullets won't re-transition unless scores change.

DEPENDENCIES:
- checkForPromotion()
- checkForDemotion()
- getEffectiveScore()
- PlaybookBullet.maturity field
- PlaybookBullet.deprecatedAt field (optional)
- PlaybookBullet.deprecationReason field (optional)

USED BY:
- curate command (end of cycle)
- Batch processing scripts
- Playbook maintenance tools","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:35.351776-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:43.804062-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qoto","title":"[Config] isSemanticallyToxic - Check against toxic log","description":"Function signature:
```typescript
async function isSemanticallyToxic(
  content: string,
  toxicLog: ToxicEntry[]
): Promise<boolean>
```

Implementation:
1. Compute content hash (SHA256)
2. Check if hash exists in toxicLog entries
3. If not found by hash, compute content embedding
4. Compare embedding against all toxic embeddings using cosine similarity
5. Return true if similarity > 0.85 threshold

ToxicEntry structure:
```typescript
interface ToxicEntry {
  contentHash: string;
  embedding: number[];
  forgottenAt: string;
  reason?: string;
}
```

Used during playbook loading to filter out bullets marked as toxic via `cm forget` command.
Prevents resurrection of harmful rules across sessions.

Performance: Hash check is O(1), embedding comparison is O(n * embedding_dim) where n = toxic entries.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:23.294264-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:18:09.470988-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-qp8","title":"[Playbook] addBullet - Add new bullet to playbook","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function addBullet(
  playbook: Playbook,
  data: NewBulletData,
  sourceSession: string
): PlaybookBullet
```

## Purpose
Add a new bullet to the playbook with complete initialization of all required fields.

## Created Bullet Structure
```typescript
{
  id: generateBulletId(),
  content: data.content,
  category: data.category,
  tags: data.tags || [],
  type: "rule",
  isNegative: false,
  state: "draft",
  maturity: "candidate",
  helpfulCount: 0,
  harmfulCount: 0,
  helpfulEvents: [],
  harmfulEvents: [],
  confidenceDecayHalfLifeDays: config.defaultDecayHalfLife || 90,
  pinned: false,
  sourceSessions: [sourceSession],
  sourceAgents: [extractAgentFromPath(sourceSession)],
  createdAt: now(),
  updatedAt: now()
}
```

## Field Initialization
- **id**: Generated via generateBulletId() (timestamp-based)
- **type**: Always "rule" by default
- **isNegative**: false (rules are positive by default)
- **state**: "draft" (new bullets start in draft)
- **maturity**: "candidate" (needs validation)
- **confidenceDecayHalfLifeDays**: From config or 90 days
- **helpfulEvents/harmfulEvents**: Empty arrays for feedback
- **sourceSessions**: Array with creating session
- **sourceAgents**: Extracted from session path

## Implementation Notes
- Push bullet to playbook.bullets array
- Return the created bullet for immediate use
- extractAgentFromPath() parses session filename
- Timestamps use ISO 8601 format

## Dependencies
- generateBulletId()
- extractAgentFromPath()
- Config for decay half-life

- findBullet() - locate created bullet
- recordFeedbackEvent() - add feedback to bullet
- savePlaybook() - persist the addition","notes":"Existing playbook helper implementations already present; no code changes made.
